Name: FPostProcessSettings
Description: To be able to use struct PostProcessSettings.

Variables.Name: SceneColorTint
Variables.Description: Scene tint color

Variables.Name: SceneFringeIntensity
Variables.Description: in percent, Scene chromatic aberration  color fringe (camera imperfection) to simulate an artifact that happens in real-world lens, mostly visible in the image corners.

Variables.Name: BloomMethod
Variables.Description: Bloom algorithm

Variables.Name: BloomIntensity
Variables.Description: Multiplier for all bloom contributions >=0: off, 1(default), >1 brighter

Variables.Name: BloomThreshold
Variables.Description:
minimum brightness the bloom starts having effect
	  -1:all pixels affect bloom equally (physically correct, faster as a threshold pass is omitted), 0:all pixels affect bloom brights more, 1(default), >1 brighter

Variables.Name: BloomSizeScale
Variables.Description: Scale for all bloom sizes

Variables.Name: Bloom1Size
Variables.Description:
Diameter size for the Bloom1 in percent of the screen width
	  (is done in 12 resolution, larger values cost more performance, good for high frequency details)
	  >=0: can be clamped because of shader limitations

Variables.Name: Bloom2Size
Variables.Description:
Diameter size for Bloom2 in percent of the screen width
	  (is done in 14 resolution, larger values cost more performance)
	  >=0: can be clamped because of shader limitations

Variables.Name: Bloom3Size
Variables.Description:
Diameter size for Bloom3 in percent of the screen width
	  (is done in 18 resolution, larger values cost more performance)
	  >=0: can be clamped because of shader limitations

Variables.Name: Bloom4Size
Variables.Description:
Diameter size for Bloom4 in percent of the screen width
	  (is done in 116 resolution, larger values cost more performance, best for wide contributions)
	  >=0: can be clamped because of shader limitations

Variables.Name: Bloom5Size
Variables.Description:
Diameter size for Bloom5 in percent of the screen width
	  (is done in 132 resolution, larger values cost more performance, best for wide contributions)
	  >=0: can be clamped because of shader limitations

Variables.Name: Bloom6Size
Variables.Description:
Diameter size for Bloom6 in percent of the screen width
	  (is done in 164 resolution, larger values cost more performance, best for wide contributions)
	  >=0: can be clamped because of shader limitations

Variables.Name: Bloom1Tint
Variables.Description: Bloom1 tint color

Variables.Name: Bloom2Tint
Variables.Description: Bloom2 tint color

Variables.Name: Bloom3Tint
Variables.Description: Bloom3 tint color

Variables.Name: Bloom4Tint
Variables.Description: Bloom4 tint color

Variables.Name: Bloom5Tint
Variables.Description: Bloom5 tint color

Variables.Name: Bloom6Tint
Variables.Description: Bloom6 tint color

Variables.Name: BloomConvolutionTexture
Variables.Description: Texture to replace default convolution bloom kernel

Variables.Name: BloomConvolutionSize
Variables.Description: Relative size of the convolution kernel image compared to the minor axis of the viewport

Variables.Name: BloomConvolutionCenterUV
Variables.Description: The UV location of the center of the kernel.  Should be very close to (.5,.5)

Variables.Name: BloomConvolutionPreFilterMin
Variables.Description: Boost intensity of select pixels  prior to computing bloom convolution (Min, Max, Multiplier).  Max < Min disables

Variables.Name: BloomConvolutionPreFilterMax
Variables.Description: Boost intensity of select pixels  prior to computing bloom convolution (Min, Max, Multiplier).  Max < Min disables

Variables.Name: BloomConvolutionPreFilterMult
Variables.Description: Boost intensity of select pixels  prior to computing bloom convolution (Min, Max, Multiplier).  Max < Min disables

Variables.Name: BloomConvolutionBufferScale
Variables.Description: Implicit buffer region as a fraction of the screen size to insure the bloom does not wrap across the screen.  Larger sizes have perf impact.

Variables.Name: BloomDirtMask
Variables.Description: Texture that defines the dirt on the camera lens where the light of very bright objects is scattered.

Variables.Name: BloomDirtMaskIntensity
Variables.Description: BloomDirtMask intensity

Variables.Name: BloomDirtMaskTint
Variables.Description: BloomDirtMask tint color

Variables.Name: RadialBlurCenterPos
Variables.Description: RadialBlur center point position

Variables.Name: RadialBlurIntensity
Variables.Description: RadialBlur intensity

Variables.Name: RadialBlurDistance
Variables.Description: RadialBlur distance from the center point

Variables.Name: AmbientCubemapTint
Variables.Description: AmbientCubemap tint color

Variables.Name: AmbientCubemapIntensity
Variables.Description:
To scale the Ambient cubemap brightness
	  >=0: off, 1(default), >1 brighter

Variables.Name: AmbientCubemap
Variables.Description: The Ambient cubemap (Affects diffuse and specular shading), blends additively which if different from all other settings here

Variables.Name: AutoExposureMethod
Variables.Description: Luminance computation method

Variables.Name: AutoExposureLowPercent
Variables.Description:
The eye adaptation will adapt to a value extracted from the luminance histogram of the scene color.
	  The value is defined as having x percent below this brightness. Higher values give bright spots on the screen more priority
	  but can lead to less stable results. Lower values give the medium and darker values more priority but might cause burn out of
	  bright spots.
	  >0, <100, good values are in the range 70 .. 80

Variables.Name: AutoExposureHighPercent
Variables.Description:
The eye adaptation will adapt to a value extracted from the luminance histogram of the scene color.
	  The value is defined as having x percent below this brightness. Higher values give bright spots on the screen more priority
	  but can lead to less stable results. Lower values give the medium and darker values more priority but might cause burn out of
	  bright spots.
	  >0, <100, good values are in the range 80 .. 95

Variables.Name: AutoExposureMinBrightness
Variables.Description:
A good value should be positive near 0. This is the minimum brightness the auto exposure can adapt to.
	  It should be tweaked in a dark lighting situation (too small: image appears too bright, too large: image appears too dark).
	  Note: Tweaking emissive materials and lights or tweaking auto exposure can look the same. Tweaking auto exposure has global
	  effect and defined the HDR range - you don't want to change that late in the project development.
	  Eye Adaptation is disabled if MinBrightness = MaxBrightness

Variables.Name: AutoExposureMaxBrightness
Variables.Description:
A good value should be positive (2 is a good value). This is the maximum brightness the auto exposure can adapt to.
	  It should be tweaked in a bright lighting situation (too small: image appears too bright, too large: image appears too dark).
	  Note: Tweaking emissive materials and lights or tweaking auto exposure can look the same. Tweaking auto exposure has global
	  effect and defined the HDR range - you don't want to change that late in the project development.
	  Eye Adaptation is disabled if MinBrightness = MaxBrightness

Variables.Name: BlindWatermarking
Variables.Description: paulshili add Blind Watermarking

Variables.Name: AutoExposureBias
Variables.Description:
Logarithmic adjustment for the exposure. Only used if a tonemapper is specified.
	  0: no adjustment, -1:2x darker, -2:4x darker, 1:2x brighter, 2:4x brighter, ...

Variables.Name: HistogramLogMin
Variables.Description: temporary exposed until we found good values, -8: 1256, -10: 11024

Variables.Name: HistogramLogMax
Variables.Description: temporary exposed until we found good values 4: 16, 8: 256

Variables.Name: LensFlareIntensity
Variables.Description: Brightness scale of the image cased lens flares (linear)

Variables.Name: LensFlareTint
Variables.Description: Tint color for the image based lens flares.

Variables.Name: LensFlareBokehSize
Variables.Description: Size of the Lens Blur (in percent of the view width) that is done with the Bokeh texture (note: performance cost is radiusradius)

Variables.Name: LensFlareCount
Variables.Description: Minimum brightness the lens flare starts having effect (this should be as high as possible to avoid the performance cost of blurring content that is too dark too see)

Variables.Name: LensFlareThreshold
Variables.Description: Minimum brightness the lens flare starts having effect (this should be as high as possible to avoid the performance cost of blurring content that is too dark too see)

Variables.Name: LensFlareBokehShape
Variables.Description: Defines the shape of the Bokeh when the image base lens flares are blurred, cannot be blended

Variables.Name: LensFlareTints
Variables.Description: RGB defines the lens flare color, A it's position. This is a temporary solution.

Variables.Name: VignetteIntensity
Variables.Description: 0..1 0=offno vignette .. 1=strong vignette

Variables.Name: GrainJitter
Variables.Description: 0..1 grain jitter

Variables.Name: GrainIntensity
Variables.Description: 0..1 grain intensity

Variables.Name: HBAOPowerExponent
Variables.Description: 0..4 >0 to enable HBAO+ (DX11Windows only) .. the greater this parameter, the darker is the HBAO

Variables.Name: HBAORadius
Variables.Description: 0..2 in meters, bigger values means even distant surfaces affect the ambient occlusion

Variables.Name: HBAOBias
Variables.Description: 0.0..0.2 increase to hide tesselation artifacts

Variables.Name: HBAODetailAO
Variables.Description: 0..1 strength of the low-range occlusion .. set to 0.0 to improve performance

Variables.Name: HBAOBlurRadius
Variables.Description: The HBAO blur is needed to hide noise artifacts .. Blur radius = 4 pixels is recommended

Variables.Name: HBAOBlurSharpness
Variables.Description: 0..32 the larger, the more the HBAO blur preserves edges

Variables.Name: HBAOMaxViewDepth
Variables.Description: depth threshold max view depth

Variables.Name: HBAODepthSharpness
Variables.Description: depth threshold sharpness in edge

Variables.Name: AmbientOcclusionIntensity
Variables.Description: 0..1 0=offno ambient occlusion .. 1=strong ambient occlusion, defines how much it affects the non direct lighting after base pass

Variables.Name: AmbientOcclusionStaticFraction
Variables.Description: 0..1 0=no effect on static lighting .. 1=AO affects the stat lighting, 0 is free meaning no extra rendering pass

Variables.Name: AmbientOcclusionRadius
Variables.Description: >0, in unreal units, bigger values means even distant surfaces affect the ambient occlusion

Variables.Name: AmbientOcclusionRadiusInWS
Variables.Description: true: AO radius is in world space units, false: AO radius is locked the view space in 400 units

Variables.Name: AmbientOcclusionFadeDistance
Variables.Description: >0, in unreal units, at what distance the AO effect disppears in the distance (avoding artifacts and AO effects on huge object)

Variables.Name: AmbientOcclusionFadeRadius
Variables.Description: >0, in unreal units, how many units before AmbientOcclusionFadeOutDistance it starts fading out

Variables.Name: AmbientOcclusionDistance_DEPRECATED
Variables.Description: >0, in unreal units, how wide the ambient occlusion effect should affect the geometry (in depth), will be removed - only used for non normal method which is not exposed

Variables.Name: AmbientOcclusionPower
Variables.Description: >0, in unreal units, bigger values means even distant surfaces affect the ambient occlusion

Variables.Name: AmbientOcclusionBias
Variables.Description: >0, in unreal units, default (3.0) works well for flat surfaces but can reduce details

Variables.Name: AmbientOcclusionQuality
Variables.Description: 0=lowest quality..100=maximum quality, only a few quality levels are implemented, no soft transition

Variables.Name: AmbientOcclusionMipBlend
Variables.Description: Affects the blend over the multiple mips (lower resolution versions) , 0:fully use full resolution, 1::fully use low resolution, around 0.6 seems to be a good value

Variables.Name: AmbientOcclusionMipScale
Variables.Description: Affects the radius AO radius scale over the multiple mips (lower resolution versions)

Variables.Name: AmbientOcclusionMipThreshold
Variables.Description: to tweak the bilateral upsampling when using multiple mips (lower resolution versions)

Variables.Name: SRTIndirectLightingColor
Variables.Description: Adjusts surfel ray tracing indirect lighting color. (1,1,1) is default.

Variables.Name: SRTIndirectLightingIntensity
Variables.Description: Scales the surfel ray tracing indirect lighting contribution. A value of 0 disables GI. Default is 1.

Variables.Name: SRTIndirectLightingSaturation
Variables.Description: Scales the surfel ray tracing indirect lighting saturation. A value of 0 left GI in only intensity. Default is 1.

Variables.Name: SRTSkyLightColor
Variables.Description: Controls the Skylight color when calculate indirect lighting.

Variables.Name: SRTSkyLightIntensity
Variables.Description: Controls the Skylight brightness scale when calculate indirect lighting.

Variables.Name: SRTBakeGIColor
Variables.Description: Controls the Skylight color when calculate indirect lighting.

Variables.Name: SRTBakeGIIntensity
Variables.Description: Controls the Skylight brightness scale when calculate indirect lighting.

Variables.Name: SRTFallbackGIColor
Variables.Description: Controls the Skylight color when there is no gi data.

Variables.Name: SRTFallbackGIIntensity
Variables.Description: Controls the Skylight brightness scale when there is no gi data.

Variables.Name: SRTMaxOcclusionDistance
Variables.Description: Scales the surfel ray tracing ambient occlusion max occlusion distance contribution. Max value is equal to sky ao.

Variables.Name: SRTMinOcclusion
Variables.Description: Controls the darkest that a fully occluded area can get.  This tends to destroy contact shadows, use Contrast or OcclusionExponent instead.

Variables.Name: SRTMinOcclusionDistance
Variables.Description: Objects farther away than this distance are considered to have a lower amount of obstruction of the skylight, which is controlled by the "SkyLight Leaking" value.

Variables.Name: SRTMinCharacterLum
Variables.Description: Increase GI intensity on character when environment is dark

Variables.Name: SRTMaxValidHitDistForBake
Variables.Description: [Use VoxelRadius As Unit] The voxel less than this distance will be accounted when tracing baked radiance. -1 means disable this restriction. Suggest less value (e.g. 4) for multiple baked local lights case.

Variables.Name: SRTLocalBakeRadianceFactor
Variables.Description: [Affect probes in given region] The value will be multiplied by original radiance when tracing baked radiance.

Variables.Name: SRTGlobalBakeRadianceFactor
Variables.Description: [Affect all baked voxels] The value will be multiplied by original radiance when injecting lightmap color to voxel.

Variables.Name: SRTFadeStartDistance
Variables.Description: Controls the fading start distance to the GI border.

Variables.Name: SRTFadeEndDistance
Variables.Description: Controls the fading end distance to the GI border.

Variables.Name: SRTDirectionalLightScale
Variables.Description: Controls the Directional Light brightness scale when calculate indirect lighting.

Variables.Name: SRTFilterSmoothness
Variables.Description: Scales the surfel ray tracing filtering scale contribution. Default is 0.2. Larger value filter more, but lost of detail indirect shadow.

Variables.Name: SRTNormalBias
Variables.Description: Apply face normal bias to 3d probe sampling position.

Variables.Name: SRTViewBias
Variables.Description: Apply view bias to 3d probe sampling position.

Variables.Name: SRTVolumePadding
Variables.Description: Fade the color near the border of the GI volume.

Variables.Name: SRTVolumeCenterElevation
Variables.Description: Apply view bias to 3d probe sampling position.

Variables.Name: SRTBentNormalAOQuality
Variables.Description: Control's bent normal AO's quality. Higher the value is, the more ray will be traced.

Variables.Name: SRTBentNormalAOIntensity
Variables.Description: Scales the surfel bent normal AO intensity. 0..1 0=offno ambient occlusion .. 1=strong ambient occlusion, defines how much it affects the indirect lighting pass of surfel GI

Variables.Name: SRTBentNormalAOPower
Variables.Description: >0, in unreal units, bigger values means even distant surfaces affect the bent normal AO.

Variables.Name: SRTBentNormalFilterKernelSize
Variables.Description: Control's bent normal AO's spatial filter's kernel size. 0 means no spatial filter.

Variables.Name: IndirectLightingColor
Variables.Description: Adjusts indirect lighting color. (1,1,1) is default. (0,0,0) to disable GI. The show flag 'Global Illumination' must be enabled to use this property.

Variables.Name: IndirectLightingIntensity
Variables.Description: Scales the indirect lighting contribution. A value of 0 disables GI. Default is 1. The show flag 'Global Illumination' must be enabled to use this property.

Variables.Name: ColorGradingIntensity
Variables.Description: Color grading lookup table intensity. 0 = no intensity, 1=full intensity

Variables.Name: ColorGradingLUT
Variables.Description: Look up table texture to use or none of not used

Variables.Name: DepthOfFieldMethod
Variables.Description: BokehDOF, Simple gaussian, ... Mobile supports Gaussian only.

Variables.Name: bMobileHQGaussian
Variables.Description: Enable HQ Gaussian on high end mobile platforms. (ES3_1)

Variables.Name: DepthOfFieldFstop
Variables.Description: CircleDOF only: Defines the opening of the camera lens, Aperture is 1fstop, typical lens go down to f1.2 (large opening), larger numbers reduce the DOF effect

Variables.Name: DepthOfFieldSensorWidth
Variables.Description: Width of the camera sensor to assume, in mm.

Variables.Name: DepthOfFieldFocalDistance
Variables.Description: Distance in which the Depth of Field effect should be sharp, in unreal units (cm)

Variables.Name: DepthOfFieldDepthBlurAmount
Variables.Description: CircleDOF only: Depth blur km for 50%

Variables.Name: DepthOfFieldDepthBlurRadius
Variables.Description: CircleDOF only: Depth blur radius in pixels at 1920x

Variables.Name: DepthOfFieldFocalRegion
Variables.Description: Artificial region where all content is in focus, starting after DepthOfFieldFocalDistance, in unreal units  (cm)

Variables.Name: DepthOfFieldNearTransitionRegion
Variables.Description: To define the width of the transition region next to the focal region on the near side (cm)

Variables.Name: DepthOfFieldFarTransitionRegion
Variables.Description: To define the width of the transition region next to the focal region on the near side (cm)

Variables.Name: DepthOfFieldScale
Variables.Description: SM5: BokehDOF only: To amplify the depth of field effect (like aperture)  0=off

Variables.Name: DepthOfFieldMaxBokehSize
Variables.Description: BokehDOF only: Maximum size of the Depth of Field blur (in percent of the view width) (note: performance cost scales with sizesize)

Variables.Name: DepthOfFieldNearBlurSize
Variables.Description: Gaussian only: Maximum size of the Depth of Field blur (in percent of the view width) (note: performance cost scales with size)

Variables.Name: DepthOfFieldFarBlurSize
Variables.Description: Gaussian only: Maximum size of the Depth of Field blur (in percent of the view width) (note: performance cost scales with size)

Variables.Name: DepthOfFieldBokehShape
Variables.Description: Defines the shape of the Bokeh when object get out of focus, cannot be blended

Variables.Name: DepthOfFieldOcclusion
Variables.Description: Occlusion tweak factor 1 (0.18 to get natural occlusion, 0.4 to solve layer color leaking issues)

Variables.Name: DepthOfFieldColorThreshold
Variables.Description: Color threshold to do full quality DOF (BokehDOF only)

Variables.Name: DepthOfFieldSizeThreshold
Variables.Description: Size threshold to do full quality DOF (BokehDOF only)

Variables.Name: DepthOfFieldSkyFocusDistance
Variables.Description: Artificial distance to allow the skybox to be in focus (e.g. 200000), <=0 to switch the feature off, only for GaussianDOF, can cost performance

Variables.Name: DepthOfFieldVignetteSize
Variables.Description: Artificial circular mask to (near) blur content outside the radius, only for GaussianDOF, diameter in percent of screen width, costs performance if the mask is used, keep Feather can Radius on default to keep it off

Variables.Name: MotionBlurAmount
Variables.Description: Strength of motion blur, 0:off, should be renamed to intensity

Variables.Name: MotionBlurMax
Variables.Description: max distortion caused by motion blur, in percent of the screen width, 0:off

Variables.Name: MotionBlurPerObjectSize
Variables.Description: The minimum projected screen radius for a primitive to be drawn in the velocity pass, percentage of screen width. smaller numbers cause more draw calls, default: 4%

Variables.Name: LPVIntensity
Variables.Description: How strong the dynamic GI from the LPV should be. 0.0 is off, 1.0 is the "normal" value, but higher values can be used to boost the effect

Variables.Name: LPVVplInjectionBias
Variables.Description: Bias applied to light injected into the LPV in cell units. Increase to reduce bleeding through thin walls

Variables.Name: LPVSize
Variables.Description: The size of the LPV volume, in Unreal units

Variables.Name: LPVSecondaryOcclusionIntensity
Variables.Description: Secondary occlusion strength (bounce light shadows). Set to 0 to disable

Variables.Name: LPVSecondaryBounceIntensity
Variables.Description: Secondary bounce light strength (bounce light shadows). Set to 0 to disable

Variables.Name: LPVGeometryVolumeBias
Variables.Description: Bias applied to the geometry volume in cell units. Increase to reduce darkening due to secondary occlusion

Variables.Name: LPVDirectionalOcclusionIntensity
Variables.Description: Controls the amount of directional occlusion. Requires LPV. Values very close to 1.0 are recommended

Variables.Name: LPVDirectionalOcclusionRadius
Variables.Description: Occlusion Radius - 16 is recommended for most scenes

Variables.Name: LPVDiffuseOcclusionExponent
Variables.Description: Diffuse occlusion exponent - increase for more contrast. 1 to 2 is recommended

Variables.Name: LPVSpecularOcclusionExponent
Variables.Description: Specular occlusion exponent - increase for more contrast. 6 to 9 is recommended

Variables.Name: LPVDiffuseOcclusionIntensity
Variables.Description: Diffuse occlusion intensity - higher values provide increased diffuse occlusion.

Variables.Name: LPVSpecularOcclusionIntensity
Variables.Description: Specular occlusion intensity - higher values provide increased specular occlusion.

Variables.Name: ScreenSpaceReflectionIntensity
Variables.Description: EnableFadedisable the Screen Space Reflection feature, in percent, avoid numbers between 0 and 1 fo consistency

Variables.Name: ScreenSpaceReflectionQuality
Variables.Description: 0=lowest quality..100=maximum quality, only a few quality levels are implemented, no soft transition, 50 is the default for better performance.

Variables.Name: ScreenSpaceReflectionMaxRoughness
Variables.Description: Until what roughness we fade the screen space reflections, 0.8 works well, smaller can run faster

Variables.Name: LPVFadeRange
Variables.Description: LPV Fade range - increase to fade more gradually towards the LPV edges.

Variables.Name: LPVDirectionalOcclusionFadeRange
Variables.Description: LPV Directional Occlusion Fade range - increase to fade more gradually towards the LPV edges.

Variables.Name: ScreenPercentage
Variables.Description:
To render with lower or high resolution than it is presented,
	 controlled by console variable,
	 100:off, needs to be <99 to get upsampling and lower to get performance,
	 >100 for super sampling (slower but higher quality),
	 only applied in game

Variables.Name: WeightedBlendables
Variables.Description:
Allows custom post process materials to be defined, using a MaterialInstance with the same Material as its parent to allow blending.
	  For materials this needs to be the "PostProcess" domain type. This can be used for any UObject object implementing the IBlendableInterface (e.g. could be used to fade weather settings).

Variables.Name: FxaaConsoleEdgeSharpness
Variables.Description: there are only three safe values here: 2 and 4 and 8: 8.0 is sharper, 4.0 is softer, 2.0 is really soft.

Variables.Name: FxaaConsoleEdgeThreshold
Variables.Description: there are only two safe values here: 14 and 18, 0.125 leaves less aliasing, but is softer, 0.25 leaves more aliasing, and is sharper.

Variables.Name: FxaaConsoleEdgeThresholdMin
Variables.Description: Trims the algorithm from processing darks: 0.06 - faster but more aliasing in darks, 0.05 - default, 0.04 - slower and less aliasing in darks.

Variables.Name: FxaaSharpeningRatio
Variables.Description: Apply sharpening method to original FXAA. 0 means no sharpening at all. 1 means fully sharpened.
